Package org.gbif.utils.file.csv

Class CSVReader

java.lang.Object
org.gbif.utils.file.csv.CSVReader
All Implemented Interfaces:
AutoCloseable, Iterator<String[]>, ClosableIterator<String[]>, ClosableReportingIterator<String[]>
public class CSVReader extends Object implements ClosableReportingIterator<String[]>

    • getHeader

      public String[] getHeader()
      Get the header, or null if none

    • close

      public void close()
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable

    • currLineNumber

      public int currLineNumber()
      Returns:
      the current line number of the String[] iterator

    • getEmptyLines

      public Set<Integer> getEmptyLines()
      Returns:
      a set of the line numbers of the firsts empty rows found in the file

    • getReadRows

      public int getReadRows()
      Returns:
      the number of rows of data that were correctly read from the file

      Specified by:
      hasNext in interface Iterator<String[]>

    • hasRowError

      public boolean hasRowError()
      Description copied from interface: ClosableReportingIterator
      Return true if an error was encountered while iterating over row.
      Specified by:
      hasRowError in interface ClosableReportingIterator<String[]>

    • getErrorMessage

      public String getErrorMessage()
      Description copied from interface: ClosableReportingIterator
      Return an informative message about the error encountered while iterating over row. Different from the Exception's message, used to store the row number, row string, etc.
      Specified by:
      getErrorMessage in interface ClosableReportingIterator<String[]>

    • getException

      public Exception getException()
      Description copied from interface: ClosableReportingIterator
      Return the Exception encountered while iterating over row.
      Specified by:
      getException in interface ClosableReportingIterator<String[]>
